senses.definition: a row of black and white lines printed on a product that contain information such as its price, readable only by a computer

examples.x: The cashier scanned the barcode on each item to ring up the customer's purchases.
The barcode on the book allowed the librarian to easily check it out to the borrower.
The warehouse manager used a handheld scanner to read the barcode on each box as it was received.
The barcode system helps retailers track inventory and manage stock levels more efficiently.
The manufacturer printed a unique barcode on each product for easy identification and tracking throughout the supply chain.

senses.definition: a label on an item that shows how much it costs

examples.x: She glanced at the price tag and was surprised by how expensive the dress was.
The price tag on the new television showed a significant discount, making it a good deal.
He carefully removed the price tags from the gifts before wrapping them for Christmas.
The price tag on the vintage collectible indicated its value had increased over the years.
She hesitated to buy the item when she saw the high price tag attached to it.

senses.definition: a small store in which fashionable clothes or accessories are sold

examples.x: She found a beautiful dress at a boutique downtown and decided to splurge on it.
The boutique specializes in handmade jewelry crafted by local artisans.
They opened a boutique bakery that sells artisanal bread and pastries.
The boutique hotel offers personalized service and stylish accommodations.
The boutique carries a curated selection of high-end fashion brands that you can't find elsewhere.

senses.definition: a room in a clothing store where people can try on items of clothing before buying them

examples.x: She tried on several outfits in the dressing room before finding the perfect one for the party.
The dressing room was equipped with full-length mirrors and comfortable seating.
The actress retreated to her dressing room to prepare for her stage performance.
The store attendant unlocked the dressing room for the customer to try on clothes.
The dressing room was bustling with activity as models prepared for the fashion show.

senses.definition: a shelf or frame with hooks or bars, etc. on which things can be put or hung

examples.x: The mechanic used a rack to lift the car off the ground so he could work underneath it.

senses.definition: money that a person can get in cash when buying something from a store with their debit card, which is then added to the bill they are paying

examples.x: The credit card offers cash-back rewards for every purchase made, providing a percentage of the total amount spent back to the cardholder.
The store advertised a cash-back promotion for customers who spent a certain amount during the holiday season.
I opted for cash-back at the checkout counter instead of using my debit card to withdraw money from the ATM.
The online retailer offers cash-back incentives for referring friends to their website.
Many banks offer cash-back bonuses for opening a new account or meeting certain requirements.

senses.definition: a machine used in restaurants, stores, etc. in which the received money is kept and each transaction is recorded

examples.x: The cashier entered the sale amount into the register and gave the customer their receipt.
The store manager balanced the register at the end of the day to ensure all transactions were accounted for.
The new register system allowed for faster checkout times and more accurate inventory tracking.
The register drawer was locked after each shift to secure the money inside.
The clerk had to call for assistance when the register froze and wouldn't process transactions.

senses.definition: a strong durable box that has a complex lock, in which people keep their valuable items

examples.x: The bank vault contained rows of safes, each belonging to a different account holder.

senses.definition: a system in which a number of cameras send their feed to television sets to protect a place and its occupants from crime

examples.x: The bank installed closed-circuit television cameras to monitor all transactions and enhance security.
The closed-circuit television system captured clear footage of the intruder breaking into the store.
Hospitals use closed-circuit television to keep an eye on critical areas and ensure patient safety.
The office building upgraded its closed-circuit television network to cover all entrances and hallways.
During the event, security personnel monitored the crowd using closed-circuit television feeds.

senses.definition: to negotiate the terms of a contract, sale, or similar arrangement for a better agreement, price, etc.

examples.x: The customers decided to bargain with the vendor to get a better price for the antique furniture.
In a business deal, both parties bargained to find a compromise on payment terms that suited their needs.
The union bargained with the company management for improved working conditions and better wages for its members.

senses.definition: to exchange goods or services without using money

examples.x: In the early days, people would barter livestock for essential goods.
The farmers decided to barter their surplus vegetables for tools from the blacksmith.
The nomadic tribes engaged in bartering furs and hides for grains and textiles.
Communities near rivers often bartered fish and other aquatic resources for agricultural produce.

senses.definition: to casually look at different products in a store with no intention of making a purchase

examples.x: On Saturday afternoons, she loves to browse through the local shops, admiring the latest fashion trends without feeling the need to make a purchase.
While waiting for his friend, he decided to browse the bookstore, flipping through new releases and enjoying the quiet atmosphere.
They often browse the mall after lunch, enjoying the window displays and catching up on the latest gadgets and styles.
Despite having no intention of buying anything, she could spend hours browsing the antique market, appreciating the history and craftsmanship of the items.
He likes to browse the electronics store to stay updated on the latest technology, even though he rarely buys anything.

senses.definition: to visit different stores to compare the price of a particular product or products before buying
to sell small quantities of goods directly to customers

examples.x: Local businesses often retail fresh produce to community members.
Small shops may retail handmade crafts to customers looking for unique items.
The company is currently retailing a new line of products to test customer interest.
Over the years, these shops have successfully retailed unique products to loyal customers.

senses.definition: to sell products in large quantities at lower prices to other stores, rather than to the public directly

examples.x: The manufacturer decided to wholesale their products to retail chains to expand their market reach.
They chose to wholesale their handmade crafts to boutiques and gift shops instead of selling directly to consumers.
The company wholesales electronics to various online retailers, who then sell them to individual customers.
By wholesaling their goods, the small business was able to reduce inventory quickly and generate consistent cash flow.

senses.definition: (of an event) to completely sell all available tickets, seats, leaving none remaining for further purchase

examples.x: The theater production sold out within minutes of the tickets going on sale.
The art exhibition sold out, and many were left disappointed without tickets.
Despite the rain, the outdoor festival sold out, attracting a large crowd.
The popular band's concert sold out within minutes of ticket sales opening.
The underground music festival sold out, transforming an abandoned warehouse into a vibrant celebration.

senses.definition: one of a series of stores that are all owned by the same company or person

examples.x: The new mall includes several chain stores, such as popular clothing and electronics retailers.
She prefers to shop at local boutiques rather than chain stores for unique fashion finds.
The chain store announced plans to open ten new locations across the state next year.
Many chain stores offer loyalty programs that reward frequent shoppers with discounts and perks.
Working at a chain store provided him with valuable retail experience and customer service skills.

senses.definition: a store that sells food, publications, alcohol, etc., often open 24 hours every day

examples.x: She stopped by the convenience store to grab a snack and a bottle of water on her way to work.
The convenience store on the corner is open 24/7, making it easy to buy essentials at any time.
The convenience store offers a variety of items, including groceries, toiletries, and lottery tickets.
During the road trip, they pulled over at a convenience store to refill their gas tank and buy some coffee.
The neighborhood convenience store is a popular spot for locals to pick up quick meals and household supplies.

senses.definition: a store that sells cheese, cooked meat, and foreign food

examples.x: She picked up a sandwich from the deli for lunch, opting for turkey and Swiss on whole wheat bread.
The deli counter offers a selection of freshly sliced meats and cheeses.
He ordered a pound of potato salad from the deli to bring to the picnic.
The deli specializes in house-made salads, sandwiches, and gourmet cheeses.
They decided to grab some bagels and lox from the deli for Sunday brunch.

senses.definition: a small store with an open front selling newspapers, etc.

examples.x: She bought a magazine from the kiosk at the train station before boarding her train.
The mall installed interactive kiosks to provide shoppers with information about store locations and promotions.
The food court has a variety of kiosks offering different types of cuisine, from Mexican to Asian.
He rented a space at the local fair to set up a kiosk selling handmade jewelry.
The airline introduced self-service check-in kiosks at the airport to streamline the boarding process.

senses.definition: a store or organization where the products of a particular company are sold at a lower price

examples.x: She loves shopping at the outlet mall because she can find brand-name clothing at discounted prices.
The company opened a new outlet store in the shopping center, offering its products at reduced prices.
They decided to visit the factory outlet to buy kitchen appliances at a fraction of the cost.
The outlet store sells last season's merchandise at a steep discount to make room for new inventory.
The online outlet website offers a wide selection of discounted items from popular brands.

senses.definition: a store that sells flowers and plants

examples.x: She visited the local florist to pick out a bouquet of flowers for her mother's birthday.
The florist specializes in custom floral arrangements for special occasions.
He stopped by the florist on his way home from work to buy a bouquet of roses for his wife.
The florist delivers fresh flowers and plants to customers' homes and businesses.
The florist on the corner of Main Street always has a stunning display of flowers in the window.

senses.definition: someone who sells food and other everyday products

examples.x: The grocer greeted each customer with a smile as they entered the store.
She stopped by the local grocer to pick up some fresh produce for dinner.
The grocer stocks a variety of organic and locally sourced foods for health-conscious shoppers.
The neighborhood grocer delivers groceries to elderly residents who are unable to shop for themselves.
He started working as a grocer at the family-owned store when he was just a teenager.

senses.definition: someone who sells writing materials, such as pens, pencils, paper, etc.

examples.x: The stationer helped me find the perfect pens and notebooks for my office.
I always seek advice from the stationer when choosing greeting cards for special occasions.
The stationer recommended a fountain pen for my calligraphy hobby.
The stationer provided excellent customer service when I needed help selecting paper for my printer.
The stationer's expertise in paper quality made it easy for me to choose the right stationery for my correspondence.

senses.definition: the idea or belief that personal well-being and happiness depend on the purchase of material goods

examples.x: The holiday season is often associated with consumerism, as people are encouraged to buy gifts for friends and family.
Critics argue that rampant consumerism is contributing to environmental degradation and social inequality.
The rise of consumerism in the 20th century led to increased production and consumption of goods.
Some people embrace minimalism as a way to resist the pressures of consumerism and live more intentionally.
Advertising plays a significant role in promoting consumerism by persuading people to buy products they may not necessarily need.

senses.definition: someone who spends a lot of time shopping, often buying unnecessary things

examples.x: She's a self-proclaimed shopaholic who can't resist buying new clothes every week.
He jokingly refers to himself as a shopaholic, always on the lookout for the latest gadgets and accessories.
The shopaholic couldn't resist the temptation of the big sale and ended up buying more than she intended.

senses.definition: (of goods) able to be imported without paying tax on them

examples.x: Travelers often take advantage of duty-free shopping at airports to purchase luxury items at discounted prices.
She bought a bottle of perfume at the duty-free shop before boarding her flight.
Duty-free stores offer a wide range of products, including alcohol, tobacco, cosmetics, and electronics.
Passengers can shop for duty-free goods while waiting for their connecting flights.
The duty-free area of the airport is popular among tourists looking for souvenirs and gifts.

senses.definition: the general process of purchasing or selling something

examples.x: The transaction of his public duties requires careful planning and execution.
She was praised for her efficient transaction of the mayoral office responsibilities.
Effective leaders ensure the seamless transaction of organizational operations.
Proper transaction of administrative duties is essential for maintaining order.
Automating the transaction of routine tasks can significantly improve efficiency.

senses.definition: available for purchase

examples.x: The popular electronics store has the latest smartphones on sale this weekend.
To celebrate the holiday season, the toy store has a wide selection of toys on sale.
The supermarket has fresh produce on sale this week.
The online tech retailer is featuring a flash sale, with various gadgets and accessories on sale for a limited time.

senses.definition: an order placed before the product is available for sale

examples.x: The store received a large number of pre-orders for the highly anticipated video game.
Pre-orders for the new iPhone surpassed expectations, with thousands of people eager to get their hands on the latest model.
The company offered a special gift for customers who placed a pre-order for the limited edition product.
She placed a pre-order for the upcoming novel by her favorite author to ensure she would receive a signed copy.
The restaurant received so many pre-orders for their Thanksgiving dinner package that they had to hire extra staff to accommodate the demand.

senses.definition: the activity of drawing public attention to a service or product in order to help it sell more

examples.x: The store is running a promotion this weekend, offering discounts on selected items.
He was thrilled to learn that he had been offered a promotion to manager of his department.
She took advantage of the promotion to upgrade her airline ticket to first class at a discounted rate.
The company invested heavily in online advertising as part of its promotion strategy for the new product launch.
The promotion campaign featured catchy slogans and eye-catching visuals to attract potential customers.

senses.definition: a digital code or a printed piece of paper that can be used instead of money when making a purchase or used to receive a discount

examples.x: The hotel provided guests with vouchers for complimentary breakfast at the onsite restaurant.
He redeemed his voucher for a free movie ticket at the theater box office.
The company offered discount vouchers to loyal customers as part of a promotional campaign.

senses.definition: reduced to half the previous price of something
the number of visitors or tourists to a shop or place during a specific period

examples.x: The coffee shop relies on foot traffic from nearby offices for its morning rush.
The shopping mall installed interactive displays to increase foot traffic and engage shoppers.
The downtown area sees a surge in foot traffic during lunchtime as people visit restaurants and cafes.
The store strategically placed its displays near the entrance to capture the attention of foot traffic passing by.
The street vendors set up their stalls along the busy sidewalk to attract foot traffic and potential customers.
